rules.title: Never commit secrets or production env files
rules.requirement: Never commit secrets, tokens, service-role keys, private certificates, or production .env files.
evidence_required: No committed secret-bearing files or values.
Any required environment contract is represented through safe examples only.

rules.title: Commit safe env examples
rules.requirement: Commit .env.example with variable names and safe placeholder values.
evidence_required: .env.example exists when environment variables are required.
Example values are safe placeholders rather than real credentials.

rules.title: Use least-privilege Supabase keys in clients
rules.requirement: Use least-privilege Supabase keys in clients. Supabase anon keys are client-safe only when Row Level Security is correct.
evidence_required: Client-side configuration uses only intended low-privilege keys.
If anon keys are used, corresponding RLS posture is reviewed.

rules.title: Keep service-role keys server-only
rules.requirement: Service role keys are server-only and must never be exposed through Vite, Expo, Next public variables, browser storage, or committed files.
evidence_required: No service-role key is exposed in public/runtime-client surfaces.
Server-only credential handling is documented where applicable.

rules.title: Require RLS on non-public Supabase tables before production use
rules.requirement: Require Row Level Security for every Supabase table that stores user, tenant, customer, financial, operational, or otherwise non-public data before production use.
evidence_required: RLS state is defined for each non-public table before production release.
Receipt or review notes identify how RLS was verified.

rules.title: Keep long-lived privileged secrets out of browser storage
rules.requirement: Keep long-lived privileged secrets out of browser storage. Store only short-lived client session material through the platform auth library unless an app-specific security review approves a different design.
evidence_required: Privileged long-lived secrets are not stored in browser storage.
Any exception has an explicit app-specific security review record.

rules.title: Treat Codex skills, hooks, and scripts as executable supply-chain artifacts
rules.requirement: Treat Codex skills, hooks, and scripts as executable supply-chain artifacts. Keep them local, reviewed, and minimal.
evidence_required: Changed executable governance surfaces are repo-local.
The change scope is reviewed and kept minimal.

rules.title: Require explicit approval for destructive or live actions
rules.requirement: Require explicit user approval before destructive filesystem commands, publishing, production deploys, or schema migrations against live databases.
evidence_required: User approval is recorded before the action runs.
Planned live or destructive operations are identified explicitly.

rules.title: Add core security and quality checks in CI beyond prototype stage
rules.requirement: Add dependency review, secret scanning, typecheck, lint, tests, and build checks in CI once a project moves beyond prototype stage.
evidence_required: CI includes dependency review, secret scanning, typecheck, lint, tests, and build checks when the project is past prototype status.
Any missing gate is called out explicitly with rationale.

rules.title: Treat production deploys as release operations
rules.requirement: Treat production deploys as release operations.
evidence_required: Production deployments are handled through release-operation discipline rather than ad hoc execution.

rules.title: Treat live database migrations as release operations
rules.requirement: Treat live database migrations as release operations.
evidence_required: Live database migrations are handled through release-operation discipline rather than ad hoc execution.

rules.title: Record release-operation details before running live deploys or migrations
rules.requirement: Record the target environment, command, expected effect, rollback path, and explicit user approval before running production deploys or live database migrations.
evidence_required: Target environment is recorded.
Exact command is recorded.
Expected effect is recorded.
Rollback path is recorded.
Explicit user approval is recorded before execution.

rules.title: Review sensitive access and policy surfaces before production readiness
rules.requirement: Review authentication, authorization, and RLS policies before production readiness.
evidence_required: Authentication, authorization, and RLS policy review is completed before production readiness.

rules.title: Review high-risk external and data-entry flows before production readiness
rules.requirement: Review payment flows, public API routes, file uploads, and AI tool actions before production readiness.
evidence_required: High-risk external and user-input flows are reviewed before production readiness.
